title: oenothera mollissima subsp. propinqua
intro: Oenothera mollissima subsp. propinqua, also known as the evening primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, and its leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of the evening primrose are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, with herbal preparations often used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
